commit.message:
Add on_new_device support and parent device via_device linking

Register devices in HA device registry via on_new_device callback,
which fires before on_new_metric in topological order. Sub-devices
(like SwitchableOutputs) are linked to their parent via via_device.

Changes:
- _on_new_device: registers devices in HA device registry proactively
- _map_device_info: uses device.parent_device for via_device instead
  of hardcoding system_0
- DeviceType check for system device instead of unique_id comparison

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[agentserver] Fix span parenting so framework child spans nest correctly (#46274)

* Fix span parenting so framework child spans nest correctly

Add set_current_span() and detach_context() to core tracing API.
Update _wrap_streaming_response in both responses and invocations
packages to re-attach the OTel span as the current context during
streaming iteration, ensuring child spans created by framework
handlers (e.g. Agent Framework) are correctly parented under the
agentserver invoke_agent span instead of appearing as siblings.

This re-applies the fixes from PRs #46068 and #46069 which were
lost during branch updates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard detach_context against out-of-order token detach

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename configure_tracing to configure_observability

Consolidate console logging and tracing setup into a single
overridable function. The default _default_configure_observability
attaches a console StreamHandler to the root logger (so user
logging.info() calls are visible) and configures OTel tracing.

Users can pass configure_observability=None to disable all
SDK-managed observability, or pass a custom function for full
control.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Consolidate observability setup into _tracing.py

Move _default_configure_observability and _CONSOLE_HANDLER_ATTR from
_base.py into _tracing.py so there is a single file for all logging
handlers, tracing exporters, and span operations.

- Renamed: configure_tracing -> _configure_tracing (internal)
- New public: configure_observability (console + tracing + OTel)
- _base.py now just imports and calls _tracing.configure_observability
- Exported configure_observability from core __init__.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci(tooling): Use GH_COPILOT_TOKEN for GitHub Models API call

The release workflow was authenticating against the GitHub Models API
with the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN, which does not carry the Copilot
entitlements needed to reach the models.github.ai endpoint. Requests
were therefore rejected with a 401/403, causing the LLM-generated
release-notes step to fall back to the minimal plaintext list on every
run.

Switching to GH_COPILOT_TOKEN — a repository secret backed by a PAT
(or a fine-grained token) that has GitHub Copilot access — gives the
workflow the correct identity. Using the default GITHUB_TOKEN was not
viable because its permission set is fixed by GitHub and cannot include
Copilot model access regardless of how the workflow's permissions block
is configured.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexandre Nicolaie <xunleii@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Remove Windows-specific dependencies from root package.json

Issue: pnpm-lock.yaml was out of sync because package.json had:
  * @esbuild/win32-x64@^0.27.3
  * @rollup/rollup-win32-x64-msvc@^4.60.1

These are Windows-only native binaries that shouldn't be in root package.json.
They caused Vercel (Linux) to fail with ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE.

Solution: Removed platform-specific dependencies from root package.json
They are auto-installed by subpackages as needed on their respective platforms.

This allows Vercel to install correctly without platform conflicts.
